<!-- GENERATED by `bun run gen` from scenarios/mysql/02-isolation/lost-update-repeatable-read.ts — do not edit by hand. -->

*The same two +10 deposits — this time at REPEATABLE READ, MySQL's default.*

```
A> BEGIN;
Query OK

A> SELECT balance FROM accounts WHERE id = 1;
 balance 
---------
     100 
(1 row)

B> BEGIN;
Query OK

B> SELECT balance FROM accounts WHERE id = 1;
 balance 
---------
     100 
(1 row)

A> UPDATE accounts SET balance = 110 WHERE id = 1;
Query OK, 1 row affected

A> COMMIT;
Query OK
```

*B's snapshot predates A's commit — but MySQL's UPDATE acts on the CURRENT row and raises nothing.*

```
B> UPDATE accounts SET balance = 110 WHERE id = 1;
Query OK, 0 rows affected

B> COMMIT;
Query OK

A> SELECT balance FROM accounts WHERE id = 1;
 balance 
---------
     110 
(1 row)
```

*PostgreSQL refuses B's write here (SQLSTATE 40001). MySQL does not — don't port that assumption.*

<small>Verified against MySQL 8.4.10 · [Run it yourself](/about/run-locally) · [Scenario source](https://github.com/svyatov/database-transactions/blob/main/scenarios/mysql/02-isolation/lost-update-repeatable-read.ts)</small>
